subject: [PATCH v3] nfsd: recall deleg if a requested dir attr change can't be encoded

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that the patch should also handle the case where the dir attribute mask is empty, as it's not clear if this is a benign or genuine failure, and requested clarification on how to distinguish between these cases.
reviews.raw_body:
Folded into "nfsd: add support to CB_NOTIFY for dir attribute changes",
thanks!

[1/1] nfsd: recall deleg if a requested dir attr change can't be encoded
      (no commit info)

--
Chuck Lever

2026-06-21.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the NFS server where directory attribute changes were silently dropped if they couldn't be encoded, leading to stale cached attributes on the client side. The patch recalls the delegation when such an error occurs, as required by RFC 8881. It also distinguishes between genuine encode failures and benign cases where no attributes are requested, ensuring that requested changes are not silently dropped.
